channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Egyptian warrior princesses, ancient avocados, and more science news
item.description: Hannah Chin and Nate Rott of NPR's Short Wave podcast talk about the building blocks of birdsong, the evolutionary history of avocados and Egyptian warrior princesses.
item.content:encoded: Hannah Chin and Nate Rott of NPR's Short Wave podcast talk about the building blocks of birdsong, the evolutionary history of avocados and Egyptian warrior princesses.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Trump cuts target federal advisory boards. What does it mean for agencies like NASA?
item.description: The Trump administration has terminated or suspended more than 100 federal advisory committees. Former members say agencies like NASA now have less accountability to stakeholders and the public.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has terminated or suspended more than 100 federal advisory committees. Former members say agencies like NASA now have less accountability to stakeholders and the public.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Invasive species cause more harm in the Global South
item.description: Invasive species can wreak havoc on ecosystems. A new study in Science suggests low- and middle-income countries in the Global South experience greater harms.
item.content:encoded: Invasive species can wreak havoc on ecosystems. A new study in Science suggests low- and middle-income countries in the Global South experience greater harms.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: In a first, Chinese woman wins the prestigious Fields Medal
item.description: Despite criticisms of a brain drain from China to the U.S., the awards come at a time when several high-profile Chinese scholars have returned home, some citing an increasingly hostile academic environment in America.
item.content:encoded: Despite criticisms of a brain drain from China to the U.S., the awards come at a time when several high-profile Chinese scholars have returned home, some citing an increasingly hostile academic environment in America.
item.dc:creator: Jan Camenzind Broomby

item.title: How a Northwest tribe and an endangered sea creature are helping each other
item.description: Scientists are starting to reintroduce a 20-legged predator to kelp beds off Washington state after an underwater pandemic nearly wiped it out.
item.content:encoded: Scientists are starting to reintroduce a 20-legged predator to kelp beds off Washington state after an underwater pandemic nearly wiped it out.
item.dc:creator: John Ryan

item.title: A strange transmissible cancer is spreading through the catfish in this lake
item.description: For more than a decade, there's been something fishy going on in a lake that straddles Vermont and the province of Quebec. It involves cancer, a kind of catfish, and — possibly — clues about how tumors metastasize.
item.content:encoded:
For more than a decade, there's been something fishy going on in a lake that straddles Vermont and the province of Quebec. It involves cancer, a kind of catfish, and — possibly — clues about how tumors metastasize.

(Image credit: Joshua Brown)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Greetings from Jerusalem, whose holy sites host some of the oldest colonies of nesting swifts
item.description: The scythe-winged birds have nested in the cracks of the Western Wall and other holy sites in Jerusalem for thousands of years.
item.content:encoded: The scythe-winged birds have nested in the cracks of the Western Wall and other holy sites in Jerusalem for thousands of years.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: How researchers found a new species of monkey deep in the rainforest
item.description: In the Democratic Republic of Congo, a team of researchers discovered a new species of monkey and formed deep friendship along the way.
item.content:encoded: In the Democratic Republic of Congo, a team of researchers discovered a new species of monkey and formed deep friendship along the way.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: An unlikely hero is saving a telescope from falling to earth
item.description: An unprecedented rescue mission is underway to save a valuable space observatory before it burns up in Earth's atmosphere.
item.content:encoded: An unprecedented rescue mission is underway to save a valuable space observatory before it burns up in Earth's atmosphere.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
item.description: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.
item.content:encoded:
Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.

(Image credit: Ruth Sherlock)
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock
